english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Imitatores mei estote, sicut et ego Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μιμηταί μου γίνεσθε καθὼς κἀγὼ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Be ye followers of me, even as I also *am* of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Laudo autem vos fratres quod per omnia mei memores estis: et sicut tradidi vobis, præcepta mea tenetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπαινῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί ὅτι πάντα μου μέμνησθε καὶ καθὼς παρέδωκα ὑμῖν τὰς παραδόσεις κατέχετε
verses.king_james: Now I praise you, brethren, that ye remember me in all things, and keep the ordinances, as I delivered *them* to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Volo autem vos scire quod omnis viri caput, Christus est: caput autem mulieris, vir: caput vero Christi, Deus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θέλω δὲ ὑμᾶς εἰδέναι ὅτι παντὸς ἀνδρὸς ἡ κεφαλὴ ὁ χριστός ἐστιν κεφαλὴ δὲ γυναικὸς ὁ ἀνήρ κεφαλὴ δὲ χριστοῦ ὁ θεός
verses.king_james: But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the head of the woman *is* the man; and the head of Christ *is* God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis vir orans, aut prophetans velato capite, deturpat caput suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ἀνὴρ προσευχόμενος ἢ προφητεύων κατὰ κεφαλῆς ἔχων καταισχύνει τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Every man praying or prophesying, having *his* head covered, dishonoureth his head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis autem mulier orans, aut prophetans non velato capite, deturpat caput suum: unum enim est ac si decalvetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα δὲ γυνὴ προσευχομένη ἢ προφητεύουσα ἀκατακαλύπτῳ τῇ κεφαλῇ καταισχύνει τὴν κεφαλὴν ἑαυτῆς ἓν γάρ ἐστιν καὶ τὸ αὐτὸ τῇ ἐξυρημένῃ
verses.king_james: But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with *her* head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is even all one as if she were shaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si non velatur mulier, tondeatur. Si vero turpe est mulieri tonderi, aut decalvari, velet caput suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ οὐ κατακαλύπτεται γυνή καὶ κειράσθω εἰ δὲ αἰσχρὸν γυναικὶ τὸ κείρασθαι ἢ ξυρᾶσθαι κατακαλυπτέσθω
verses.king_james: For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir quidem non debet velare caput suum: quoniam imago et gloria Dei est, mulier autem gloria viri est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνὴρ μὲν γὰρ οὐκ ὀφείλει κατακαλύπτεσθαι τὴν κεφαλήν εἰκὼν καὶ δόξα θεοῦ ὑπάρχων γυνὴ δὲ δόξα ἀνδρός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: For a man indeed ought not to cover *his* head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim vir ex muliere est, sed mulier ex viro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ἀνὴρ ἐκ γυναικός ἀλλὰ γυνὴ ἐξ ἀνδρός
verses.king_james: For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim non est creatus vir propter mulierem, sed mulier propter virum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ οὐκ ἐκτίσθη ἀνὴρ διὰ τὴν γυναῖκα ἀλλὰ γυνὴ διὰ τὸν ἄνδρα
verses.king_james: Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo debet mulier potestatem habere supra caput propter angelos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ὀφείλει ἡ γυνὴ ἐξουσίαν ἔχειν ἐπὶ τῆς κεφαλῆς διὰ τοὺς ἀγγέλους
verses.king_james: For this cause ought the woman to have power on *her* head because of the angels.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verumtamen neque vir sine muliere: neque mulier sine viro in Domino.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν οὔτε ἀνὴρ χωρὶς γυναικὸς οὔτε γυνὴ χωρὶς ἀνδρὸς ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Nevertheless neither is the man without the woman, neither the woman without the man, in the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam sicut mulier de viro, ita et vir per mulierem: omnia autem ex Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ γὰρ ἡ γυνὴ ἐκ τοῦ ἀνδρός οὕτως καὶ ὁ ἀνὴρ διὰ τῆς γυναικός τὰ δὲ πάντα ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For as the woman *is* of the man, even so *is* the man also by the woman; but all things of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos ipsi judicate: decet mulierem non velatam orare Deum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ὑμῖν αὐτοῖς κρίνατε πρέπον ἐστὶν γυναῖκα ἀκατακάλυπτον τῷ θεῷ προσεύχεσθαι
verses.king_james: Judge in yourselves: is it comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec ipsa natura docet vos, quod vir quidem si comam nutriat, ignominia est illi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ οὐδὲ αὐτὴ ἤ φύσις διδάσκει ὑμᾶς ὅτι ἀνὴρ μὲν ἐὰν κομᾷ ἀτιμία αὐτῷ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long hair, it is a shame unto him?

verses.latin_vulgate: mulier vero si comam nutriat, gloria est illi: quoniam capilli pro velamine ei dati sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γυνὴ δὲ ἐὰν κομᾷ δόξα αὐτῇ ἐστιν ὅτι ἡ κόμη ἀντὶ περιβολαίου δέδοται αὐτῇ
verses.king_james: But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for *her* hair is given her for a covering.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem videtur contentiosus esse: nos talem consuetudinem non habemus, neque ecclesia Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις δοκεῖ φιλόνεικος εἶναι ἡμεῖς τοιαύτην συνήθειαν οὐκ ἔχομεν οὐδὲ αἱ ἐκκλησίαι τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But if any man seem to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc autem præcipio: non laudans quod non in melius, sed in deterius convenitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ παραγγέλλων οὐκ ἐπαινῶ ὅτι οὐκ εἰς τὸ κρεῖττον ἀλλ᾽ εἰς τὸ ἧττον συνέρχεσθε
verses.king_james: Now in this that I declare *unto you* I praise *you* not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.

verses.latin_vulgate: Primum quidem convenientibus vobis in ecclesiam, audio scissuras esse inter vos, et ex parte credo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρῶτον μὲν γὰρ συνερχομένων ὑμῶν ἐν τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ ἀκούω σχίσματα ἐν ὑμῖν ὑπάρχειν καὶ μέρος τι πιστεύω
verses.king_james: For first of all, when ye come together in the church, I hear that there be divisions among you; and I partly believe it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam oportet et hæreses esse, ut et qui probati sunt, manifesti fiant in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῖ γὰρ καὶ αἱρέσεις ἐν ὑμῖν εἶναι ἵνα οἱ δόκιμοι φανεροὶ γένωνται ἐν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: For there must be also heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convenientibus ergo vobis in unum, jam non est Dominicam cœnam manducare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνερχομένων οὖν ὑμῶν ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ οὐκ ἔστιν κυριακὸν δεῖπνον φαγεῖν
verses.king_james: When ye come together therefore into one place, *this* is not to eat the Lord's supper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unusquisque enim suam cœnam præsumit ad manducandum, et alius quidem esurit, alius autem ebrius est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕκαστος γὰρ τὸ ἴδιον δεῖπνον προλαμβάνει ἐν τῷ φαγεῖν καὶ ὃς μὲν πεινᾷ ὃς δὲ μεθύει
verses.king_james: For in eating every one taketh before *other* his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid domos non habetis ad manducandum, et bibendum? aut ecclesiam Dei contemnitis, et confunditis eos qui non habent? Quid dicam vobis? laudo vos? in hoc non laudo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γὰρ οἰκίας οὐκ ἔχετε εἰς τὸ ἐσθίειν καὶ πίνειν ἢ τῆς ἐκκλησίας τοῦ θεοῦ καταφρονεῖτε καὶ καταισχύνετε τοὺς μὴ ἔχοντας τί ὑμῖν εἴπω ἐπαινέσω ὑμᾶς ἐν τούτῳ οὐκ ἐπαινῶ
verses.king_james: What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the church of God, and shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise *you* not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego enim accepi a Domino quod et tradidi vobis, quoniam Dominus Jesus in qua nocte tradebatur, accepit panem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ γὰρ παρέλαβον ἀπὸ τοῦ κυρίου ὃ καὶ παρέδωκα ὑμῖν ὅτι ὁ κύριος ἰησοῦς ἐν τῇ νυκτὶ ᾗ παρεδίδοτο ἔλαβεν ἄρτον
verses.king_james: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the *same* night in which he was betrayed took bread:

verses.latin_vulgate: et gratias agens fregit, et dixit: Accipite, et manducate: hoc est corpus meum, quod pro vobis tradetur: hoc facite in meam commemorationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐχαριστήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ εἶπεν λάβετε, φάγετε, τοῦτό μου ἐστὶν τὸ σῶμα τὸ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν κλώμενον τοῦτο ποιεῖτε εἰς τὴν ἐμὴν ἀνάμνησιν
verses.king_james: And when he had given thanks, he brake *it*, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similiter et calicem, postquam cœnavit, dicens: Hic calix novum testamentum est in meo sanguine: hoc facite quotiescumque bibetis, in meam commemorationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡσαύτως καὶ τὸ ποτήριον μετὰ τὸ δειπνῆσαι λέγων, τοῦτο τὸ ποτήριον ἡ καινὴ διαθήκη ἐστὶν ἐν τῷ ἐμῷ αἵματι τοῦτο ποιεῖτε ὁσάκις ἂν πίνητε εἰς τὴν ἐμὴν ἀνάμνησιν
verses.king_james: After the same manner also *he took* the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink *it*, in remembrance of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quotiescumque enim manducabitis panem hunc, et calicem bibetis, mortem Domini annuntiabitis donec veniat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁσάκις γὰρ ἂν ἐσθίητε τὸν ἄρτον τοῦτον καὶ τὸ ποτήριον τοῦτο πίνητε τὸν θάνατον τοῦ κυρίου καταγγέλλετε ἄχρις οὗ ἂν ἔλθῃ
verses.king_james: For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord's death till he come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque quicumque manducaverit panem hunc, vel biberit calicem Domini indigne, reus erit corporis et sanguinis Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ὃς ἂν ἐσθίῃ τὸν ἄρτον τοῦτον ἢ πίνῃ τὸ ποτήριον τοῦ κυρίου ἀναξίως ἔνοχος ἔσται τοῦ σώματος καὶ αἵματος τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: Wherefore whosoever shall eat this bread, and drink *this* cup of the Lord, unworthily, shall be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Probet autem seipsum homo: et sic de pane illo edat, et de calice bibat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δοκιμαζέτω δὲ ἄνθρωπος ἑαυτόν καὶ οὕτως ἐκ τοῦ ἄρτου ἐσθιέτω καὶ ἐκ τοῦ ποτηρίου πινέτω
verses.king_james: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of *that* bread, and drink of *that* cup.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim manducat et bibit indigne, judicium sibi manducat et bibit, non dijudicans corpus Domini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ ἐσθίων καὶ πίνων ἀναξίως, κρίμα ἑαυτῷ ἐσθίει καὶ πίνει μὴ διακρίνων τὸ σῶμα τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: For he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord's body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo inter vos multi infirmi et imbecilles, et dormiunt multi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἐν ὑμῖν πολλοὶ ἀσθενεῖς καὶ ἄῤῥωστοι καὶ κοιμῶνται ἱκανοί
verses.king_james: For this cause many *are* weak and sickly among you, and many sleep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si nosmetipsos dijudicaremus, non utique judicaremur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἑαυτοὺς διεκρίνομεν οὐκ ἂν ἐκρινόμεθα
verses.king_james: For if we would judge ourselves, we should not be judged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum judicamur autem, a Domino corripimur, ut non cum hoc mundo damnemur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κρινόμενοι δὲ ὑπὸ κυρίου παιδευόμεθα ἵνα μὴ σὺν τῷ κόσμῳ κατακριθῶμεν
verses.king_james: But when we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque fratres mei, cum convenitis ad manducandum, invicem exspectate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε ἀδελφοί μου συνερχόμενοι εἰς τὸ φαγεῖν ἀλλήλους ἐκδέχεσθε
verses.king_james: Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis esurit, domi manducet, ut non in judicium conveniatis. Cetera autem, cum venero, disponam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις πεινᾷ ἐν οἴκῳ ἐσθιέτω ἵνα μὴ εἰς κρίμα συνέρχησθε τὰ δὲ λοιπὰ ὡς ἂν ἔλθω διατάξομαι
verses.king_james: And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.
